Wits - +3 Mental on Non Power uses dealing with focus.

rows: When you were living, the occult was simply one of those interesting things people dabbled in at parties. Of course, perhaps you believed it more than most, you could see the spirits of the dead. You used to think you were mad, but you quickly figured out how to parlay that into a bit of a business for yourself. You were a hit on the elite party scene in glittering New York, and made quite the name for yourself in the papers. Perhaps that's how the Tremere found you. Your abilities intrigued them, and they brought you into the clan as much for you ability to see ghosts as for your ability to hob knob with the best in society. New York for Kindred at the time was firmly a Sabbat city, but the Tremere were as entrenched there as the other sect, with the powerful Five Burroughs Chantry serving as one of the main hubs of the clan. Your influence with the elite of society went pretty far to ensure that. How could anyone think to tear down that darling, occult bookstore when you and your friends loved to gather there? Your influence with the elite ensured the clan some measure of protection over the decades until such time as the Camarilla was able to assert itself in the city once again. In the years since, the Camarilla has shifted both in leadership and outlook. Your skills were useful once, particularly amongst the keepers and harpies and other social officers of the court, but as the Toreador and Ventrue have moved in, a Tremere in that position was slowly pushed to the side. What is more, your clan in the city has had to change focus as well. Still just as powerful as it ever was, the focus on the greater chantry rather than the politics of the domain has left you without support. However, word has gotten to you of the happenings in Los Angeles. There are many whispers of what is going on there with the clan, and who might be planning what, but you know there is a Tremere prince in the city and a court in a war zone where a young, ambitious Tremere with just as much style and clout as any Toreador could make a difference. Besides, you've heard there are more mediums and spiritualists than you can shake a stick at, and perhaps there would be others to connect with, others you could maybe form a coterie with. There are many possibilities here for you in a new city, and youa re eager to find out what they are.
Your relationship to these people is described. This may mean they do not know you.

Necromancy has been the sole purview of the Giovanni, who jealously guard their power. But in researching Los Angeles, you have discovered that the Malkavian elder of the city is an open practitioner of these arts!

A Toreador elder and socialite without equal. To make the proper social splash in the city, you'll need to know the right people. Start with Alvarado, and get her to introduce you to the power actors in Los Angeles.

In your studies of alternative styles of magic, you've heard of hints of some sort of rare Nordic magic, and the name 'Elof' keeps coming up. Find out what sort of magic this Gangrel elder might know about - or even have access to!

Necromancy has always been a difficult power for the Tremere to obtain. See if you can be the one to bridge the gap between Thaumaturgy and Necromancy; either by learning Necromancy yourself or developing the Thaumaturgy to help you replicate it.
Too many of the other clans view the Tremere with fear and disdain. Demonstrate that the Tremere can be as socially adept as anyone else. Bring a sense of style and panache to the clan!
Attain a court position for yourself - something social, like an Assistant Harpy or Keeper of Elysium.
